deep_dive.title: SA-RA-JSCC: SNR-Adaptive and Semantic-Rate-Aware Joint Source-Channel Coding
deep_dive.abstract: In joint source-channel coding (JSCC)-based semantic communication systems, achieving stable and reliable image semantic transmission under channel constraints remains a key challenge. In most channel adaptation modules, the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) is often injected into each layer of a channel-adaptation model in an independent and layer-wise manner, which undermines global coordination across layers. Therefore, consistent noise-robust representations may fail to be learned throughout the model. To address this problem, we propose SA-RA-JSCC, a novel channel-adaptive JSCC model. SA-RA-JSCC maps SNR into a unified semantic vector in the feature space and then applies a one-shot global reweighting to the encoded features, thereby enabling globally consistent and learnable channel adaptation. Moreover, in order to further enhance the anti-channel capability of semantic information, a semantic-rate-aware module is introduced, enabling the adaptive policy to respond simultaneously to fluctuations in channel quality and changes in semantic-rate constraints, thereby enhancing global network coordination and channel adaptivity. Extensive experiment results across multiple channels and datasets demonstrate that SA-RA-JSCC significantly outperforms existing semantic communication models in terms of reconstruction metrics such as PSNR and MS-SSIM, exhibiting stronger robustness across a broad range of SNR regimes.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: End-to-end joint source-channel coding with rate-distortion optimization for semantic image transmission
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 端到端联合信源信道编码，带有率失真优化的语义图像传输
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 端到端联合信源信道编码，带有率失真优化的语义图像传输
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 端到端联合信源信道编码，带有率失真优化的语义图像传输
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: SA-RA-JSCC proposes a joint source-channel coding model with global SNR reweighting for robust image semantic transmission under channel constraints.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出SA-RA-JSCC模型，将信噪比映射为统一语义向量并对编码特征进行全局重加权，实现信道自适应的端到端图像语义传输，在保持语义保真度的同时提升抗噪声鲁棒性。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: end-to-end learned image compression with rate-distortion optimization

quick_skim.title: Can Neural Networks Achieve Optimal Computational-statistical Tradeoff? An Analysis on Single-Index Model
quick_skim.abstract: In this work, we tackle the following question: Can neural networks trained with gradient-based methods achieve the optimal computational-statistical tradeoff in learning Gaussian single-index models? Prior research has shown that any polynomial-time algorithm under the statistical query (SQ) framework requires $Ω(d^{s^\star/2}\lor d)$ samples, where $s^\star$ is the generative exponent representing the intrinsic difficulty of learning the underlying model. However, it remains unknown whether neural networks can achieve this sample complexity. Inspired by prior techniques such as label transformation and landscape smoothing for learning single-index models, we propose a unified gradient-based algorithm for training a two-layer neural network in polynomial time. Our method is adaptable to a variety of loss and activation functions, covering a broad class of existing approaches. We show that our algorithm learns a feature representation that strongly aligns with the unknown signal $θ^\star$, with sample complexity $\widetilde{O} (d^{s^\star/2} \lor d)$, matching the SQ lower bound up to a polylogarithmic factor for all generative exponents $s^\star\geq 1$. Furthermore, we extend our approach to the setting where $θ^\star$ is $k$-sparse for $k = o(\sqrt{d})$ by introducing a novel weight perturbation technique that leverages the sparsity structure. We derive a corresponding SQ lower bound of order $\widetildeΩ(k^{s^\star})$, matched by our method up to a polylogarithmic factor. Our framework, especially the weight perturbation technique, is of independent interest, and suggests potential gradient-based solutions to other problems such as sparse tensor PCA.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: analyzes computational-statistical tradeoff in neural network training with gradient methods, relevant to optimization strategies
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 分析了神经网络训练的统计计算权衡，涉及梯度优化策略
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 分析了神经网络训练的统计计算权衡，涉及梯度优化策略
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 分析了神经网络训练的统计计算权衡，涉及梯度优化策略
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Investigates whether neural networks can achieve optimal sample complexity in learning single-index models via gradient methods, proposing a unified algorithm that matches the SQ lower bound.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对神经网络能否达到最优样本复杂度这一理论问题，分析了高斯单索引模型的学习。提出了一种基于梯度方法的统一算法，通过标签变换和景观平滑技术，在理论上证明了其样本复杂度与统计查询下界匹配。实验验证了算法的有效性。这项工作为理解深度学习的计算-统计权衡提供了重要理论支撑。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对神经网络能否达到最优样本复杂度这一理论问题，分析了高斯单索引模型的学习。提出了一种基于梯度方法的统一算法，通过标签变换和景观平滑技术，在理论上证明了其样本复杂度与统计查询下界匹配。实验验证了算法的有效性。这项工作为理解深度学习的计算-统计权衡提供了重要理论支撑。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: model optimization strategies for deep learning

quick_skim.title: Show the Signal, Hide the Noise: Spectral Forcing for Pixel-Space Diffusion
quick_skim.abstract: Pixel-space diffusion models are trained on full-bandwidth noisy images, yet the useful signal available to the denoiser is strongly frequency dependent. Under rectified-flow diffusion and natural-image power-law spectra, the per-band data-to-noise contour $k^{*}(t) = (1-t)^{-2/α}$ separates a signal-bearing low-frequency region from a noise-dominated high-frequency region at each time $t$. We show that this implicit coarse-to-fine structure is not merely descriptive: it induces a capacity-allocation problem. A standard pixel-space denoiser must discover the moving bandwidth boundary internally and can spend computation on frequency-time regions where the optimal prediction collapses to deterministic baselines rather than data-distribution modeling. To make this boundary explicit, we introduce Spectral Forcing, a parameter-free, time-conditional 2D-DCT low-pass operator applied to the noisy input before the patch embedder. Its cutoff expands monotonically with the diffusion time and becomes the identity at the data endpoint. Through controlled synthetic experiments, we identify the regime in which the operator is beneficial: coarse patch tokenization and data whose high-frequency content is predominantly noise rather than essential signal. On ImageNet-256 with JiT-700M/32, Spectral Forcing consistently improves both FID and Inception Score across different training epochs, demonstrating robust gains throughout training; at finer tokenization, the spectral forcing is still competitive. We further insert the unchanged operator into SenseNova-U1, a unified text-to-image model, where it improves DPG-Bench and GenEval, showing that the input-side spectral prior transfers beyond class-conditional generation. These results suggest a route to capacity-efficient pixel-space diffusion by showing the signal and hiding the noise.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: spectral analysis and frequency decomposition in diffusion models share concepts with transform coding
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 扩散模型中的频谱分析与频率分解与变换编码概念相通
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 扩散模型中的频谱分析与频率分解与变换编码概念相通
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 扩散模型中的频谱分析与频率分解与变换编码概念相通
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper reveals the implicit coarse-to-fine structure in pixel-space diffusion via spectral forcing, highlighting a capacity-allocation problem that could inform transform coding design.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文揭示了像素空间扩散模型中的隐式粗到细结构，通过谱强制方法展示了频率相关的信噪比边界。这导致了一个容量分配问题：标准去噪器可能在确定性区域浪费计算资源。该发现对于理解频域分解和优化计算分配具有启发意义，可能与变换编码中的频带处理相关。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文揭示了像素空间扩散模型中的隐式粗到细结构，通过谱强制方法展示了频率相关的信噪比边界。这导致了一个容量分配问题：标准去噪器可能在确定性区域浪费计算资源。该发现对于理解频域分解和优化计算分配具有启发意义，可能与变换编码中的频带处理相关。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 图像压缩方向论文检索, especially work that connects or combines: transform coding approaches such as DCT and wavelet for image compression; quantization strategies in image compression pipelines; neural network based image compression models using autoencoders; end-to-end learned image compression with rate-distortion optimization; neural network architectures for end to end lossy image compression; rate distortion optimization methods in learned image compression.

quick_skim.title: VL2Spike: Spike-driven Distillation from VLMs for Low-Power Visual Perception in Embodied AI
quick_skim.abstract: Spiking neural networks (SNNs) are brain-inspired, event-driven models that compute with sparse spikes, which enables highly efficient visual perception in resource-constrained embodied AI models. The emergence of Spiking-Transformer models with spike self-attention has substantially improved the learning capacity of pure SNNs. Although SNNs are energy efficient, their performance is still limited by the spike-based architecture and optimization challenges, as standard gradient descent rules cannot be directly applied. Recently, vision-language models (VLMs) have shown rich multi-modal knowledge representation capabilities for visual perception. Thus, it is promising to leverage VLMs for better Spikformer training. To this end, we present VL2Spike, a novel spike-based knowledge distillation (KD) framework that bridges multi-modal knowledge from VLMs with compact Spikformer models. This design enhances the learning capacity of Spikformer models while preserving their energy-efficiency merits, thereby offering a practical pathway toward low-power robotic perception. Our VL2Spike brings two key technical contributions. To align with spiking dynamics, we first propose spatial-temporal visual spike (SVS) distillation, which achieves (1) shared manifold alignment between VLM image features and spike tokens, and (2) warm-started temporal consistency on membrane potentials and spike rates. We then design a novel spike prototype-guided linguistic (SPL) distillation strategy that aligns Spikformer's class prototypes and logits with promptable VLM text embeddings. Extensive experiments show that VL2Spike achieves 6.81% gain across three static datasets with only 15.7% energy consumption. It also exhibits strong generalization capacity on robotic visual place recognition (VPR) with a gain of 6.63%, highlighting its potential for low-power perception in embodied AI.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: spike-driven distillation from VLMs for low-power visual perception, improving vision architecture
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 从VLM进行脉冲驱动蒸馏用于低功耗视觉感知，改进视觉架构
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 从VLM进行脉冲驱动蒸馏用于低功耗视觉感知，改进视觉架构
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 从VLM进行脉冲驱动蒸馏用于低功耗视觉感知，改进视觉架构
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper leverages vision-language models to distill knowledge into spiking neural networks for efficient visual perception, advancing architecture design.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文利用视觉语言模型（VLM）将知识蒸馏到脉冲神经网络（SNN）中，以实现低功耗视觉感知。针对SNN优化困难的问题，提出利用VLM的多模态表示能力来训练Spikformer。该方法提升了SNN的性能，为视觉架构设计提供了新的蒸馏范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文利用视觉语言模型（VLM）将知识蒸馏到脉冲神经网络（SNN）中，以实现低功耗视觉感知。针对SNN优化困难的问题，提出利用VLM的多模态表示能力来训练Spikformer。该方法提升了SNN的性能，为视觉架构设计提供了新的蒸馏范式。

quick_skim.title: MamBOA: State-Space Architecture for Video Recognition
quick_skim.abstract: Fine-grained action recognition demands temporal reasoning that general-purpose architectures address through different cost-accuracy tradeoffs: 3D dense operators couple computation to the input volume, while difference-based methods approximate motion through rigid, hand-crafted subtraction of uncontextualized features - each reflecting a deliberate design choice with corresponding limitations in expressiveness or flexibility. We present MamBOA, a backbone-agnostic temporal framework built upon a novel interleaved scan structure that recasts the selective state-space recurrence (S6) as a native motion synthesizer. By interleaving consecutive feature representations extracted from a pretrained backbone into a single alternating sequence, the proposed scan structurally drives the recurrence to encode both temporal observations of each position within a shared hidden state, separated by only a single decay step - rendering the inter-frame transition an intrinsic component of the state dynamics rather than an externally computed quantity. A cascade of dedicated alignment and decoding operations then distills this joint encoding into an explicit motion representation, which a dual-path pooling mechanism adaptively aggregates by balancing attention-driven selection with uniform temporal coverage. The framework interfaces seamlessly with CNN, Transformer, and Mamba backbone families, adding only ~2.1 GFLOPs per feature pair. On Diving48, MamBOA achieves 85.02% Top-1 accuracy with an image-pretrained backbone and 86.24% with a video-pretrained backbone processing the entire video in a single forward pass - demonstrating that structurally induced state-space dynamics constitute a principled and general foundation for motion modeling.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: novel state-space architecture for video recognition, improving temporal reasoning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于视频识别的新型状态空间架构，改进时序推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于视频识别的新型状态空间架构，改进时序推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于视频识别的新型状态空间架构，改进时序推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MamBOA presents a backbone-agnostic temporal framework using interleaved scans and state-space recurrence for fine-grained action recognition.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出MamBOA，一种与骨干无关的时序框架，通过交错扫描结构和状态空间递归（S6）实现运动合成。该方法在细粒度动作识别上取得了更好的成本-精度权衡，为视频识别架构设计提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出MamBOA，一种与骨干无关的时序框架，通过交错扫描结构和状态空间递归（S6）实现运动合成。该方法在细粒度动作识别上取得了更好的成本-精度权衡，为视频识别架构设计提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: Optimizing Multiple Feature Types for Image Inpainting in the Linear and Nonlinear Setting
quick_skim.abstract: Inpainting-based compression stores a carefully optimized subset of the full image data and reconstructs the missing data by inpainting. The quality of these lossy codecs depends decisively on the stored data. So far, these data consist almost exclusively of pixel locations along with their grayscale or color values. In the present paper, we present a general theory and a practical framework that allows to incorporate arbitrary features which can be described by linear or nonlinear equations. This includes e.g. derivatives of arbitrary order or local integrals. Our features can be combined with linear or nonlinear inpainting operators. Moreover, we present an algorithm that automatically optimizes the location and the type of the selected feature. The approach of allowing different types of optimized features turns inpainting-based compression into a more general, versatile and powerful paradigm. Our experiments report a consistent quality gain when increasing the number of feature types from 1 to 5. With the same amount of stored data, the average peak signal-to-noise improvement is 2.76 dB for harmonic (homogeneous diffusion) inpainting, and 1.82 dB for edge-enhancing diffusion inpainting.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Proposes optimizing multiple feature types for inpainting-based image compression.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出优化多种特征类型用于基于修补的图像压缩。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出优化多种特征类型用于基于修补的图像压缩。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出优化多种特征类型用于基于修补的图像压缩。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper presents a general theory and framework for optimizing arbitrary features in inpainting-based compression, improving codec quality.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有基于修补的图像压缩仅优化像素值的局限，提出了一个通用理论框架，允许融合如导数、局部积分等任意特征，并自动优化特征位置和类型，显著提升了有损编解码器的重建质量。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对现有基于修补的图像压缩仅优化像素值的局限，提出了一个通用理论框架，允许融合如导数、局部积分等任意特征，并自动优化特征位置和类型，显著提升了有损编解码器的重建质量。

quick_skim.title: Variable-Width Transformers
quick_skim.abstract: Scaling model size, specifically depth and width, has driven significant progress in transformer-based language models. However, most architectures maintain a constant width across all layers, allocating a fixed parameter and computation budget evenly despite different layers potentially playing distinct computational roles. In this work, we empirically investigate nonuniform capacity allocation across network depth by proposing a $\times$-shaped > <former architecture. This design maintains wider early and late layers while narrowing the middle layers, utilizing a parameter-free residual resizing mechanism. Across decoder-only language models ranging from 200M to 2B parameters (dense) and 3B parameters (MoE), our > <former consistently outperforms parameter-matched uniform baselines on language modeling loss. By reducing the average layer width, this architecture also requires fewer overall FLOPs (22% reduction under fitted loss-matched scaling curves) and smaller KV cache memory and I/O cost (15% reduction). In analysis, we show that this bottleneck structure results in qualitatively different representations in residual streams. Overall, our results demonstrate that nonuniform width allocation can result in more resource-optimal scaling of language models.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: nonuniform width allocation across transformer layers, a best practice for architecture design
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 跨Transformer层的非均匀宽度分配，一种架构设计最佳实践
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 跨Transformer层的非均匀宽度分配，一种架构设计最佳实践
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 跨Transformer层的非均匀宽度分配，一种架构设计最佳实践
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a variable-width transformer (><former) with wider early/late and narrower middle layers, outperforming uniform-width baselines on language models; methodology transferable to vision.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 大多数Transformer在所有层使用恒定宽度，未能区分不同层的计算角色。本文提出变宽Transformer（><former），保持早期和晚期层宽、中间层窄，利用参数无关的残差调整。在200M到2B参数的密集和MoE模型上，始终优于均匀宽度基线。该非均匀分配思想可启发视觉Transformer架构设计，提升参数效率。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 大多数Transformer在所有层使用恒定宽度，未能区分不同层的计算角色。本文提出变宽Transformer（><former），保持早期和晚期层宽、中间层窄，利用参数无关的残差调整。在200M到2B参数的密集和MoE模型上，始终优于均匀宽度基线。该非均匀分配思想可启发视觉Transformer架构设计，提升参数效率。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: best practices for neural network architecture design

quick_skim.title: ITNet: A Learnable Integral Transform That Subsumes Convolution, Attention, and Recurrence
quick_skim.abstract: Convolutional networks, recurrent networks, and transformers each encode different inductive biases -- locality, sequential memory, and content-dependent pairwise interaction -- and have remained mathematically distinct since their inception. We show that this fragmentation reflects not a fundamental diversity in how signals should be processed, but rather incomplete views of a single underlying mathematical object: a learnable integral transform. We introduce the Integral Transform Network (ITNet), a unified architecture built around a learnable kernel that depends jointly on positions and features. This kernel is implemented as a small neural network, specifically an MLP, that models pairwise interactions, enabling the model to adapt its behavior from data. We show that convolution, self-attention (including multi-head), and autoregressive recurrence (including LSTM, GRU, S4, and Mamba) arise as special cases under appropriate parameterizations, and that ITNet is a universal approximator of continuous operators. To make this practical, we develop tiled kernel fusion, importance-weighted Monte Carlo integration, and learned low-rank factorization, enabling efficient and scalable computation. A single ITNet architecture with a shared operator and lightweight modality-specific encoders matches or exceeds specialized baselines on ImageNet-1K , GLUE, ModelNet40, VQA\,v2 and NLVR2. The results demonstrate that a single learned interaction mechanism can recover the behavior of all three architectural families from data.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes unified architecture ITNet subsuming conv, attention, recurrence
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出统一架构ITNet，融合卷积、注意力与循环
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出统一架构ITNet，融合卷积、注意力与循环
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出统一架构ITNet，融合卷积、注意力与循环
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ITNet introduces a learnable integral transform kernel implemented as MLP, unifying convolution, attention, and recurrence into a single architecture.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有神经网络架构碎片化，每种架构编码不同的归纳偏置。ITNet提出可学习积分变换，用一个MLP实现的核同时建模位置和特征依赖，统一了卷积、注意力和循环网络。在多种任务上展示了竞争力，为架构设计提供了新视角。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有神经网络架构碎片化，每种架构编码不同的归纳偏置。ITNet提出可学习积分变换，用一个MLP实现的核同时建模位置和特征依赖，统一了卷积、注意力和循环网络。在多种任务上展示了竞争力，为架构设计提供了新视角。

quick_skim.title: Addressing Detail Bottlenecks in Latent Diffusion for RGB-to-SWIR Image Translation
quick_skim.abstract: Latent diffusion models (LDMs) enable efficient image-to-image translation but discard fine spatial details during compression, degrading downstream perception tasks. We identify two bottlenecks: the autoencoder, which loses spatial information, and the conditioning pathway, which further degrades the source signal through naive downsampling. We propose two lightweight, backbone-agnostic fixes: a Source-Conditioned Autoencoder (SCAE) that injects high-resolution source features into the decoder via skip connections, and a Learnable Guidance Encoder (LGE) that replaces naive downsampling with a learned conditioning signal. Evaluated on RGB-to-SWIR translation for driving scenes with two denoiser backbones (U-Net and DiT), our approach improves detection mAP by up to 2x over the latent diffusion baseline, with up to 3.4x gains on small objects (COCO-small, <32^2 px^2), while achieving state-of-the-art FID. We further show that FID and detection performance are poorly correlated, motivating multi-axis evaluation. Results generalise zero-shot to the public RASMD benchmark. We will publicly release test data with annotations, all checkpoints, and training code.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Addresses autoencoder compression bottlenecks in latent diffusion models, relevant to neural network-based image compression using autoencoders
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 解决潜在扩散模型中自编码器的压缩瓶颈，与基于自编码器的神经网络图像压缩相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 解决潜在扩散模型中自编码器的压缩瓶颈，与基于自编码器的神经网络图像压缩相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 解决潜在扩散模型中自编码器的压缩瓶颈，与基于自编码器的神经网络图像压缩相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Source-Conditioned Autoencoder (SCAE) and Learnable Guidance Encoder to recover spatial details lost during compression in latent diffusion models for image translation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对潜在扩散模型在图像翻译中因压缩丢失空间细节的问题，提出源条件自编码器（SCAE）通过跳跃连接注入高分辨率特征，以及可学习引导编码器（LGE）替代朴素下采样，在保持轻量级的同时显著提升下游感知任务性能。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对潜在扩散模型在图像翻译中因压缩丢失空间细节的问题，提出源条件自编码器（SCAE）通过跳跃连接注入高分辨率特征，以及可学习引导编码器（LGE）替代朴素下采样，在保持轻量级的同时显著提升下游感知任务性能。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: neural network based image compression models using autoencoders

quick_skim.title: Linear Recurrent Unit with Semantic Modulation for Image Super-Resolution
quick_skim.abstract: Linear recurrent unit (LRU), designed with a principled formulation for stable linear recurrence, has demonstrated promising accuracy and robustness on long-range dependency tasks. However, its static parameterization and single-scan method limits its applicability to 2D vision tasks. In this study, we propose a LRU-based restoration network with a semantic modulating unit (SMU) to achieve a harmonious balance between performance and efficiency in single-image super-resolution. The SMU plays three key roles: LRU modulation, spatial categorization, and feature enhancement through learned prototype. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method quantitatively and qualitatively surpasses recent state-of-the-art methods. Notably, our approach achieves superior performance with computational complexity on par with existing methods. The source code and models are available at https://github.com/MingyuChoi-run/LSM
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LRU with semantic modulation for image super-resolution, a neural architecture improvement technique
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于图像超分辨率的经语义调制的LRU，一种神经网络架构改进技术
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于图像超分辨率的经语义调制的LRU，一种神经网络架构改进技术
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于图像超分辨率的经语义调制的LRU，一种神经网络架构改进技术
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes LRU-based restoration network with Semantic Modulating Unit (SMU) for efficient image super-resolution, surpassing state-of-the-art methods.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 图像超分辨率需要平衡性能和效率。本文提出基于线性循环单元（LRU）和语义调制单元（SMU）的恢复网络，SMU承担LRU调制、空间分类和基于原型的特征增强三种角色。实验表明该方法在定量和定性上超越最新方法，且在效率上具有竞争力，为视觉任务设计高效循环架构提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 图像超分辨率需要平衡性能和效率。本文提出基于线性循环单元（LRU）和语义调制单元（SMU）的恢复网络，SMU承担LRU调制、空间分类和基于原型的特征增强三种角色。实验表明该方法在定量和定性上超越最新方法，且在效率上具有竞争力，为视觉任务设计高效循环架构提供了新思路。

quick_skim.title: Spatial-Aware Reduction Framework: Towards Efficient and Faithful Visual State Space Models
quick_skim.abstract: Mamba demonstrates strong efficiency in modeling long visual sequences. However, when token reduction is applied to structurally enhanced Mamba variants, these models exhibit a severe performance collapse. We attribute this degradation to the spatially agnostic nature of existing reduction methods, which violate the two-dimensional structural premise required by the selective scanning mechanism. In this work, we propose STORM, a spatial-aware token reduction framework designed to maintain structural integrity throughout the compression process. STORM reformulates reduction into a structured operation on spatial units, enforcing localized constraints to maintain both grid topology and neighborhood coherence. As a plug-and-play module, STORM equips existing reduction pipelines with explicit spatial awareness without any training. Empirical results demonstrate that STORM achieves state-of-the-art pruning accuracy across diverse vision Mamba backbones under training-free settings. Notably, STORM delivers a substantial accuracy recovery on VMamba, outperforming prior methods by up to 63.3\% in top-1 accuracy. Meanwhile, STORM incurs only a 1.0\% accuracy drop on PlainMamba, achieving performance comparable to ViT.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: computer vision architecture improvement through spatial-aware token reduction for state space models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过空间感知令牌缩减改进视觉状态空间模型架构
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过空间感知令牌缩减改进视觉状态空间模型架构
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过空间感知令牌缩减改进视觉状态空间模型架构
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: STORM proposes spatial-aware token reduction for visual SSMs, maintaining structural integrity and preventing performance collapse.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文指出视觉Mamba模型在令牌缩减时性能崩溃源于空间感知缺失，提出STORM框架，将缩减重构为基于空间单元的结构化操作，保持网格拓扑和邻域一致性。作为即插即用模块，STORM在多个视觉任务上恢复并提升效率，为高效视觉状态空间模型设计提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文指出视觉Mamba模型在令牌缩减时性能崩溃源于空间感知缺失，提出STORM框架，将缩减重构为基于空间单元的结构化操作，保持网格拓扑和邻域一致性。作为即插即用模块，STORM在多个视觉任务上恢复并提升效率，为高效视觉状态空间模型设计提供了新思路。
